classes.name: discord_flask.models.connections.UserConnection

methods.name: discord_flask.models.connections.UserConnection.is_visible
documentation: A property returning bool if this integration is visible to everyone.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.connections.UserConnection.fetch_from_api
documentation:
A class method which returns an instance or list of instances of this model by implicitly making an
API call to Discord. If an instance of {py:class}`discord_flask.User` exists in the users internal cache
who are attached to these connections then, the cached property {py:attr}`discord_flask.User.connections`
is updated.

# Parameters

cache

: Determines if the {py:attr}`discord_flask.User.guilds` cache should be updated with the new guilds.

# Returns

list\[discord_flask.UserConnection, ...\]

: List of instances of {py:class}`discord_flask.UserConnection` to which this user belongs.

Class representing connections in discord account of the user.

Attributes
----------
id : str
    ID of the connection account.
name : str
    The username of the connection account.
type : str
    The service of connection (twitch, youtube).
revoked : bool
    A boolean representing whether the connection is revoked.
integrations : list
    A list of server Integration objects.
verified : bool
    A boolean representing whether the connection is verified.
friend_sync : bool
    A boolean representing whether friend sync is enabled for this connection.
show_activity : bool
    A boolean representing whether activities related to this connection will
    be shown in presence updates.
visibility : int
    An integer representing
    `visibility <https://discordapp.com/developers/docs/resources/user#user-object-visibility-types>`_
    of this connection.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.channel.Channel.send
documentation:
Send a message to the channel.

Parameters
----------
content : str
    Content of the message.
embed : discord.Embed, optional
    Embed to be sent with the message.

Returns
-------
discord.Message
    Message object of the sent message.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.base.DiscordModelsBase._request
documentation:
A shorthand to :py:func:discord_flask.request`. It uses Flask current_app local proxy to get the
discord_flask client.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.base.DiscordModelsBase._bot_request
documentation: A shorthand to :py:func:discord_flask.bot_request`.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.base.DiscordModelsBase.fetch_from_api
documentation:
A class method which returns an instance or list of instances of this model by implicitly making an
API call to Discord.

Returns
-------
cls
    An instance of this model itself.
[cls, ...]
    List of instances of this model when many of these models exist.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.base.DiscordModelsBase.to_json
documentation:
A utility method which returns raw payload object as it was received from discord.

Returns
-------
dict
    A dict representing raw payload object received from discord.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.role.Role.__init__
documentation: print(json.dumps(_payload, indent=4))

classes.name: discord_flask.models.user.User

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.guilds
documentation:
A cached mapping of user's guild ID to {py:class}`discord_flask.Guild`. The guilds are cached when the first
API call for guilds is requested so it might be an empty dict.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.name
documentation: An alias to the username attribute.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.avatar_url
documentation: A property returning direct URL to user's avatar.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.default_avatar_url
documentation: A property which returns the default avatar URL as when user doesn't has any avatar set.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.is_avatar_animated
documentation: A boolean representing if avatar of user is animated. Meaning user has GIF avatar.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.fetch_from_api
documentation:
A class method which returns an instance of this model by implicitly making an
API call to Discord. The user returned from API will always be cached and update in internal cache.

# Parameters

guilds

: A boolean indicating if user's guilds should be cached or not. Defaults to `False`. If chose to not
  cache, user's guilds can always be obtained from {py:func}`discord_flask.Guilds.fetch_from_api()`.

connections

: A boolean indicating if user's connections should be cached or not. Defaults to `False`. If chose to not
  cache, user's connections can always be obtained from {py:func}`discord_flask.Connections.fetch_from_api()`.

# Returns

cls

: An instance of this model itself.

\[cls, ...\]

: List of instances of this model when many of these models exist.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.get_from_cache
documentation:
A class method which returns an instance of this model if it exists in internal cache.

Returns
-------
discord_flask.User
    An user instance if it exists in internal cache.
None
    If the current doesn't exists in internal cache.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.add_to_guild
documentation:
Method to add user to the guild, provided OAuth2 session has already been created with `guilds.join` scope.

# Parameters

guild_id

: The ID of the guild you want this user to be added.

# Returns

dict

: A dict of guild member object. Returns an empty dict if user is already present in the guild.

# Raises

discord_flask.Unauthorized

: Raises {py:class}`discord_flask.Unauthorized` if current user is not authorized.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.fetch_guilds
documentation:
A method which makes an API call to Discord to get user's guilds. It prepares the internal guilds cache
and returns list of all guilds the user is member of.

# Returns

list

: List of {py:class}`discord_flask.Guilds` instances.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.user.User.fetch_connections
documentation:
A method which makes an API call to Discord to get user's connections. It prepares the internal connection
cache and returns list of all connection instances.

# Returns

list

: A list of {py:class}`discord_flask.UserConnection` instances.

Class representing Discord User.

# Operations

x == y

: Checks if two user's are the same.

x != y

: Checks if two user's are not the same.

str(x)

: Returns the user's name with discriminator.

# Attributes

id

: The discord ID of the user.

username

: The discord username of the user.

discriminator

: 4 length string representing discord tag of the user.

avatar_hash

: Hash of users avatar.

bot

: A boolean representing whether the user belongs to an OAuth2 application.

mfa_enabled

: A boolean representing whether the user has two factor enabled on their account.

locale

: The user's chosen language option.

verified

: A boolean representing whether the email on this account has been verified.

email

: User's email ID.

flags

: An integer representing the
  [user flags](https://discordapp.com/developers/docs/resources/user#user-object-user-flags).

premium_type

: An integer representing the
  [type of nitro subscription](https://discordapp.com/developers/docs/resources/user#user-object-premium-types).

connections

: A list of {py:class}`discord_flask.UserConnection` instances. These are cached and this list might be empty.

classes.name: discord_flask.models.user.Bot
documentation: Class representing the client user itself.

classes.name: discord_flask.models.guild.CGuild

methods.name: discord_flask.models.guild.CGuild.icon_url
documentation: A property returning direct URL to the guild's icon. Returns None if guild has no icon set.

methods.name: discord_flask.models.guild.CGuild.fetch_from_api
documentation:
A class method which returns an instance or list of instances of this model by implicitly making an
API call to Discord. If an instance of {py:class}`discord_flask.User` exists in the users internal cache
who belongs to these guilds then, the cached property {py:attr}`discord_flask.User.guilds` is updated.

# Parameters

cache

: Determines if the {py:attr}`discord_flask.User.guilds` cache should be updated with the new guilds.

# Returns

list\[discord_flask.Guild, ...\]

: List of instances of {py:class}`discord_flask.Guild` to which this user belongs.

CGuild Class representing discord Guild the user is part of.

Operations
----------
x == y
    Checks if two guild's are the same.
x != y
    Checks if two guild's are not the same.
str(x)
    Returns the guild's name.

Attributes
----------
id : int
    Discord ID of the guild.
name : str
    Name of the guild.
icon_hash : str
    Hash of guild's icon.
is_owner : bool
    Boolean determining if current user is owner of the guild or not.
permissions : discord.Permissions
    An instance of discord.Permissions representing permissions of current user in the guild.

A decorator for flask views which raises exception {py:class}`flaskcord.Unauthorized` if the user
is not authorized from Discord OAuth2.

classes.name: discord_flask.client.Session

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.create_session
documentation:
Primary method used to create OAuth2 session and redirect users for
authorization code grant.

Parameters
----------
scope : list, optional
    An optional list of valid `Discord OAuth2 Scopes
    <https://discordapp.com/developers/docs/topics/oauth2#shared-resources-oauth2-scopes>`_.
data : dict, optional
    A mapping of your any custom data which you want to access after authorization grant. Use
    `:py:meth:discord_flask.Session.callback` to retrieve this data in your callback view.
prompt : bool, optional
    Determines if the OAuth2 grant should be explicitly prompted and re-approved. Defaults to True.
    Specify False for implicit grant which will skip the authorization screen and redirect to redirect URI.
permissions: typing.Union[discord_flask.types.Permissions, int], optional
    An optional parameter determining guild permissions of the bot while adding it to a guild using
    discord OAuth2 with `bot` scope. It is same as generating so called *bot invite link* which redirects
    to your callback endpoint after bot authorization flow. Defaults to 0 or no permissions.
params : kwargs, optional
    Additional query parameters to append to authorization URL for customized OAuth flow.

Returns
-------
redirect
    Flask redirect to discord authorization servers to complete authorization code grant process.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.save_authorization_token
documentation:
A staticmethod which saves a dict containing Discord OAuth2 token and other secrets to the user's cookies.
Meaning by default, it uses client side session handling.

Override this method if you want to handle the user's session server side. If this method is overridden then,
you must also override {py:meth}`discord_flask.Session.get_authorization_token`.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.get_authorization_token
documentation:
A static method which returns a dict containing Discord OAuth2 token and other secrets which was saved
previously by `` :py:meth:`discord_flask.Session.save_authorization_token `` from user's cookies.

You must override this method if you are implementing server side session handling.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.callback
documentation:
A method which should be always called after completing authorization code grant process
usually in callback view.
It fetches the authorization token and saves it flask
`session <http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/api/#flask.session>`_ object.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.revoke
documentation:
This method clears current discord token, state and all session data from flask
`session <http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/api/#flask.session>`_. Which means user will have
to go through discord authorization token grant flow again. Also tries to remove the user from internal
cache if they exist.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.authorized
documentation: A boolean indicating whether current session has authorization token or not.

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.fetch_user
documentation:
This method returns user object from the internal cache if it exists otherwise makes an API call to do so.

Returns
-------
discord_flask.models.User

methods.name: discord_flask.client.Session.fetch_connections
documentation:
This method returns list of user connection objects from internal cache if it exists otherwise
makes an API call to do so.

# Returns

list

: List of {py:class}`discord_flask.models.UserConnection` objects.

Main client class representing hypothetical OAuth2 session with discord.
It uses Flask [session](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/api/#flask.session) local proxy object
to save state, authorization token and keeps record of users sessions across different requests.
This class inherits {py:class}`discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient` class.

# Parameters

app

: An instance of your [flask application](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/api/#flask.Flask).

client_id

: The client ID of discord application provided. Can be also set to flask config
  with key `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID`.

client_secret

: The client secret of discord application provided. Can be also set to flask config
  with key `DISCORD_CLIENT_SECRET`.

redirect_uri

: The default URL to use to redirect user to after authorization. Can be also set to flask config
  with key `DISCORD_REDIRECT_URI`.

bot_token

: The bot token of the application. This is required when you also need to access bot scope resources
  beyond the normal resources provided by the OAuth. Can be also set to flask config with
  key `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`.

users_cache

: Any dict like mapping to internally cache the authorized users. Preferably an instance of
  cachetools.LFUCache or cachetools.TTLCache. If not specified, default cachetools.LFUCache is used.
  Uses the default max limit for cache if `DISCORD_USERS_CACHE_MAX_LIMIT` isn't specified in app config.

# Attributes

client_id

: The client ID of discord application provided.

redirect_uri

: The default URL to use to redirect user to after authorization.

users_cache

: A dict like mapping to internally cache the authorized users. Preferably an instance of
  cachetools.LFUCache or cachetools.TTLCache. If not specified, default cachetools.LFUCache is used.
  Uses the default max limit for cache if `DISCORD_USERS_CACHE_MAX_LIMIT` isn't specified in app config.

modules.name: discord_flask._http
documentation: This code is mostly copied from flask_discord
Author: https://github.com/weibeu

classes.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient.init_app
documentation:
A method to lazily initialize the application.
Use this when you're using flask factory pattern to create your instances of your flask application.

Parameters
----------
app : Flask
    An instance of your `flask application <http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/api/#flask.Flask>`_.

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient.user_id
documentation:
A property which returns Discord user ID if it exists in flask {py:attr}`flask.session` object.

# Returns

int

: The Discord user ID of current user.

None

: If the user ID doesn't exists in flask {py:attr}`flask.session`.

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient._make_session
documentation:
A low level method used for creating OAuth2 session.

Parameters
----------
token : str, optional
    The authorization token to use which was previously received from authorization code grant.
state : str, optional
    The state to use for OAuth2 session.
scope : list, optional
    List of valid `Discord OAuth2 Scopes
    <https://discordapp.com/developers/docs/topics/oauth2#shared-resources-oauth2-scopes>`_.

Returns
-------
OAuth2Session
    An instance of OAuth2Session class.

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient.request
documentation:
Sends HTTP request to provided route or discord endpoint.

# Note

It automatically prefixes the API Base URL so you will just have to pass routes or URL endpoints.

# Parameters

route

: Route or endpoint URL to send HTTP request to. Example: `/users/@me`

method

: Specify the HTTP method to use to perform this request.

data

: The optional payload the include with the request.

oauth

: A boolean determining if this should be Discord OAuth2 session request or any standard request.

# Returns

dict, str

: Dictionary containing received from sent HTTP GET request if content-type is `application/json`
  otherwise returns raw text content of the response.

# Raises

discord_flask.Unauthorized

: Raises {py:class}`discord_flask.Unauthorized` if current user is not authorized.

discord_flask.RateLimited

: Raises an instance of {py:class}`discord_flask.RateLimited` if application is being rate limited by Discord.

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient.bot_request
documentation:
Make HTTP request to specified endpoint with bot token as authorization headers.

# Parameters

route

: Route or endpoint URL to send HTTP request to.

method

: Specify the HTTP method to use to perform this request.

# Returns

dict, str

: Dictionary containing received from sent HTTP GET request if content-type is `application/json`
  otherwise returns raw text content of the response.

# Raises

discord_flask.Unauthorized

: Raises {py:class}`discord_flask.Unauthorized` if current user is not authorized.

discord_flask.RateLimited

: Raises an instance of {py:class}`discord_flask.RateLimited` if application is being rate limited by Discord.

methods.name: discord_flask._http.DiscordOAuth2HttpClient.send_message
documentation: Send a message to specified channel.

An OAuth2 http abstract base class providing some factory methods.
This class is meant to be overridden by {py:class}`discord_flask.Session` and should not be
used directly.

classes.name: discord_flask.exceptions.HttpException
documentation: Base Exception class representing a HTTP exception.

classes.name: discord_flask.exceptions.RateLimited
documentation:
A HTTP Exception raised when the application is being rate limited.
It provides the `response` attribute which can be used to get more details of the actual response from
the Discord API with few more shorthands to `response.json()`.

# Attributes

json

: The actual JSON data received. Shorthand to `response.json()`.

message

: A message saying you are being rate limited.

retry_after

: The number of milliseconds to wait before submitting another request.

is_global

: A value indicating if you are being globally rate limited or not

classes.name: discord_flask.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation: A HTTP Exception raised when user is not authorized.

classes.name: discord_flask.exceptions.AccessDenied
documentation: Exception raised when user cancels OAuth authorization grant.

So the PermissionOverwrite knows to work with it

classes.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.__init__
documentation: noinspection PyMissingConstructor

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.is_subset
documentation: Returns `True` if self has the same or fewer permissions as other.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.is_superset
documentation: Returns `True` if self has the same or more permissions as other.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.is_strict_subset
documentation: Returns `True` if the permissions on other are a strict subset of those on self.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.is_strict_superset
documentation: Returns `True` if the permissions on other are a strict superset of those on self.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.none
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
permissions set to `False`.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.all
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
permissions set to `True`.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.all_channel
documentation:
A {class}`Permissions` with all channel-specific permissions set to
`True` and the guild-specific ones set to `False`. The guild-specific
permissions are currently:

- {attr}`manage_emojis`
- {attr}`view_audit_log`
- {attr}`view_guild_insights`
- {attr}`manage_guild`
- {attr}`change_nickname`
- {attr}`manage_nicknames`
- {attr}`kick_members`
- {attr}`ban_members`
- {attr}`administrator`

:::{versionchanged} 1.7 Added :attr:`stream`, :attr:`priority_speaker` and :attr:`use_slash_commands` permissions.
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.general
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"General" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionchanged} 1.7 Permission :attr:`read_messages` is now included in the general permissions, but permissions :attr:`administrator`, :attr:`create_instant_invite`, :attr:`kick_members`, :attr:`ban_members`, :attr:`change_nickname` and :attr:`manage_nicknames` are no longer part of the general permissions.
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.membership
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Membership" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.text
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Text" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionchanged} 1.7 Permission :attr:`read_messages` is no longer part of the text permissions. Added :attr:`use_slash_commands` permission.
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.voice
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Voice" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.stage
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Stage Channel" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.stage_moderator
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Stage Moderator" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.advanced
documentation:
A factory method that creates a {class}`Permissions` with all
"Advanced" permissions from the official Discord UI set to `True`.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.update
documentation:
Bulk updates this permission object.

Allows you to set multiple attributes by using keyword
arguments. The names must be equivalent to the properties
listed. Extraneous key/value pairs will be silently ignored.

Parameters
------------
\*\*kwargs
    A list of key/value pairs to bulk update permissions with.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.create_instant_invite
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if the user can create instant invites.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.kick_members
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if the user can kick users from the guild.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.ban_members
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can ban users from the guild.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.administrator
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user is an administrator. This role overrides all other permissions.

This also bypasses all channel-specific overrides.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_channels
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can edit, delete, or create channels in the guild.

This also corresponds to the "Manage Channel" channel-specific override.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_guild
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can edit guild properties.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.add_reactions
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can add reactions to messages.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.view_audit_log
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can view the guild's audit log.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.priority_speaker
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can be more easily heard while talking.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.stream
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can stream in a voice channel.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.read_messages
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can read messages from all or specific text channels.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.view_channel
documentation:
{class}`bool`: An alias for {attr}`read_messages`.

:::{versionadded} 1.3
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.send_messages
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can send messages from all or specific text channels.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.send_tts_messages
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can send TTS messages from all or specific text channels.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_messages
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can delete or pin messages in a text channel.

:::{note}
Note that there are currently no ways to edit other people's messages.
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.embed_links
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user's messages will automatically be embedded by Discord.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.attach_files
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can send files in their messages.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.read_message_history
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can read a text channel's previous messages.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.mention_everyone
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user's @everyone or @here will mention everyone in the text channel.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.external_emojis
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can use emojis from other guilds.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.use_external_emojis
documentation:
{class}`bool`: An alias for {attr}`external_emojis`.

:::{versionadded} 1.3
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.view_guild_insights
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can view the guild's insights.

:::{versionadded} 1.3
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.connect
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can connect to a voice channel.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.speak
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can speak in a voice channel.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.mute_members
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can mute other users.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.deafen_members
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can deafen other users.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.move_members
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can move users between other voice channels.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.use_voice_activation
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can use voice activation in voice channels.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.change_nickname
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can change their nickname in the guild.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_nicknames
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can change other user's nickname in the guild.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_roles
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can create or edit roles less than their role's position.

This also corresponds to the "Manage Permissions" channel-specific override.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_permissions
documentation:
{class}`bool`: An alias for {attr}`manage_roles`.

:::{versionadded} 1.3
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_webhooks
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can create, edit, or delete webhooks.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.manage_emojis
documentation: {class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can create, edit, or delete emojis.

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.use_slash_commands
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can use slash commands.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

methods.name: discord_flask.types.permissions.Permissions.request_to_speak
documentation:
{class}`bool`: Returns `True` if a user can request to speak in a stage channel.

:::{versionadded} 1.7
:::

Wraps up the Discord permission value.

The properties provided are two way. You can set and retrieve individual
bits using the properties as if they were regular bools. This allows
you to edit permissions.

:::{versionchanged} 1.3 You can now use keyword arguments to initialize :class:`Permissions` similar to :meth:`update`.
:::

:::{container} operations
```
.. describe:: x == y

    Checks if two permissions are equal.
```

```
.. describe:: x != y

    Checks if two permissions are not equal.
```

```
.. describe:: x <= y

    Checks if a permission is a subset of another permission.
```

```
.. describe:: x >= y

    Checks if a permission is a superset of another permission.
```

```
.. describe:: x < y

     Checks if a permission is a strict subset of another permission.
```

```
.. describe:: x > y

     Checks if a permission is a strict superset of another permission.
```

```
.. describe:: hash(x)

       Return the permission's hash.
```

```
.. describe:: iter(x)

       Returns an iterator of ``(perm, value)`` pairs. This allows it
       to be, for example, constructed as a dict or a list of pairs.
       Note that aliases are not shown.
```
:::

# Attributes

value: {class}`int`

: The raw value. This value is a bit array field of a 53-bit integer
  representing the currently available permissions. You should query
  permissions via the properties rather than using this raw value.
